title: TISHCHENKO REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Tishchenko reaction describing dimerization or crossed coupling of aldehydes to esters through hydride transfer catalyzed by metal alkoxides or related Lewis acidic systems.
notes: Covers traditional, crossed, aldol-Tishchenko, and Evans-Tishchenko variants with emphasis on aldehyde disproportionation to esters and 1,3-diol monoester formation.

title: TISHCHENKO REACTION
summary: Application page for the Tishchenko reaction highlighting Evans-Tishchenko and related aldol-Tishchenko strategies in the syntheses of sarain A, rapamycin, and rhizoxin D.
notes: Examples emphasize SmI2-mediated anti-selective monoester formation and tandem aldol/Tishchenko sequences in complex natural product synthesis.

title: TSUJI-TROST REACTION / ALLYLATION
summary: Overview page for the Tsuji-Trost allylation describing Pd-catalyzed substitution of allylic electrophiles by soft or hard nucleophiles through π-allylpalladium intermediates.
notes: Focuses on allylic substitution, regioselectivity with soft versus hard nucleophiles, and retention or inversion trends in Pd-mediated allylation chemistry.

title: TSUJI-TROST REACTION / ALLYLATION
summary: Application page for the Tsuji-Trost reaction showing intramolecular and intermolecular allylations in the syntheses of FR182877, biotin, cristatc acid, and amphidinolide K fragments.
notes: Examples highlight carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, and sulfone-derived soft nucleophiles in allylic substitution during target-oriented synthesis.

title: TSUJI-WILKINSON DECARBONYLATION REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Tsuji-Wilkinson decarbonylation describing RhCl(PPh3)3-mediated removal of formyl groups from aldehydes and acyl halides to give decarbonylated hydrocarbons or halides.
notes: Emphasizes Wilkinson catalyst promoted decarbonylation of aldehydes, acyl halides, and 1,2-diketones with retention of stereochemistry at the formyl-bearing center.

title: TSUJI-WILKINSON DECARBONYLATION REACTION
summary: Application page for the Tsuji-Wilkinson decarbonylation featuring formyl-group removal in the syntheses of FR-900482 core, neuroactive benzo[e]indenes, gomisin J, and aphanamol I.
notes: Representative examples show selective deformylation late in synthesis using RhCl(PPh3)3 under reflux conditions.

title: UGI MULTICOMPONENT REACTION
summary: Overview page for the Ugi multicomponent reaction describing four-component coupling of amines, carbonyl compounds, isocyanides, and nucleophiles such as carboxylic acids to furnish α-acylamino amides and related products.
notes: Highlights the classical Ugi four-component reaction together with hydantoin, thiourea, and tetrazole-producing variants derived from alternative acidic components.

title: UGI MULTICOMPONENT REACTION
summary: Application page for the Ugi multicomponent reaction showing use in the syntheses of furanomycin, ecteinascidin 743 fragments, and ketopiperazine libraries.
notes: Examples emphasize rapid multicomponent assembly of peptide-like and alkaloid-oriented scaffolds followed by deprotection or cyclization.

title: ULLMANN BIARYL ETHER AND BIARYL AMINE SYNTHESIS / CONDENSATION
summary: Overview page for Ullmann biaryl ether and biaryl amine synthesis describing Cu-mediated coupling of aryl halides with phenols or arylamines to form biaryl ethers and diarylamines.
family_names: Ullmann Biaryl Ether and Biaryl Amine Synthesis / Condensation
reference_family_name: Ullmann Biaryl Ether and Biaryl Amine Synthesis / Condensation
notes: Summarizes classical high-temperature Ullmann conditions and modified Cu(I)/Cu(II)-mediated protocols for ether, amine, and sulfide bond formation.

title: ULLMANN BIARYL ETHER AND BIARYL AMINE SYNTHESIS / CONDENSATION
summary: Application page for Ullmann condensation featuring macrocyclizations and biaryl ether/amine formation in the syntheses of combretastatin D-2, diepoxin σ, vancomycin model systems, and SB-214857.
family_names: Ullmann Biaryl Ether and Biaryl Amine Synthesis / Condensation
reference_family_name: Ullmann Biaryl Ether and Biaryl Amine Synthesis / Condensation
notes: Examples showcase intramolecular and intermolecular Cu-mediated aryl O- and N-couplings under both classical and modified Ullmann conditions.
